steps.title: Express 10,000 as a power of 10
steps.content:
We have $g(x) = 10^{4x-2} = 10,000$. To solve this exponential equation, rewrite 10,000 as a power of 10:

$$10,000 = 10^4$$

So the equation becomes:

$$10^{4x-2} = 10^4$$

steps.title: Equate exponents and solve for x
steps.content:
Since both sides have the same base (10), the exponents must be equal:

$$4x - 2 = 4$$

Add 2 to both sides:

$$4x = 6$$

Divide by 4:

$$x = \frac{6}{4} = \frac{3}{2}$$

That matches choice B — B is correct.
